title: Red Hat Security Advisory: shadow-utils security update
overview:
An update for shadow-utils is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Low. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

full_text:
Red Hat Security Advisory: shadow-utils security update

An update for shadow-utils is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Low. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

An update for shadow-utils is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Low. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

Updated Packages:
shadow-utils-4.9-15.el9.src.rpm
shadow-utils-4.9-15.el9.x86_64.rpm
shadow-utils-debuginfo-4.9-15.el9.i686.rpm

shadow-utils-subid-debuginfo-4.9-15.el9.ppc64le.rpm
shadow-utils-4.9-15.el9.aarch64.rpm
shadow-utils-debuginfo-4.9-15.el9.aarch64.rpm
shadow-utils-debugsource-4.9-15.el9.aarch64.rpm
shadow-utils-subid-4.9-15.el9.aarch64.rpm
shadow-utils-subid-debuginfo-4.9-15.el9.aarch64.rpm
shadow-utils-subid-devel-4.9-15.el9.i686.rpm
shadow-utils-subid-devel-4.9-15.el9.x86_64.rpm
shadow-utils-subid-devel-4.9-15.el9.ppc64le.rpm
shadow-utils-subid-devel-4.9-15.el9.aarch64.rpm

CVE-2024-56433: A flaw was found in shadow-utils. Affected versions of shadow-utils establish a default /etc/subuid behavior, for example, uid 100000 through 165535 for the first user account, that can conflict with the uids of users defined on locally administered networks. This issue potentially leads to account takeover by leveraging newuidmap for access to an NFS home directory or same-host resources for remote logins by these local network users.
